nodes.label: Monster_Encounter
nodes.description: A monstrous creature climbs aboard the ship's deck.

nodes.label: Tribute Demand
nodes.description: A speaker claims these waters belong to the Scaled Queen and asserts their right to collect tribute

nodes.label: Dialogue_with_Kobolds
nodes.description: Kobolds barrage the travelers with questions upon learning their identity

nodes.label: Ship Crash Discovery
nodes.description: Rix witnesses a ship crashing on the northern rocks

nodes.label: Tattoo Symbol Recognition
nodes.description: Character identifies the gold hangman's noose tattoo as a symbol of the Gilded Gallows thieves' guild.

nodes.label: Blocked Mushroom Harvest
nodes.description: Tarak's attempt to gather heart cap mushrooms from myconids is thwarted by a fungal octopus guardian.

nodes.label: discovery_of_plaza
nodes.description: The path widens into a long plaza with six open doorways carved into the cliff wall

nodes.label: discovery_of_statue
nodes.description: A massive stone statue of a dragon overlooks the plaza

nodes.label: Description
nodes.description: Text describes monastic cells layout and furnishings

nodes.label: Zombie Encounter
nodes.description: Adventurers fight three zombies threatening Blepp and Tarak

nodes.label: Dialogue with Runara
nodes.description: Runara reveals the wrecked ship as the zombie source

nodes.label: Investigation Quest
nodes.description: Characters tasked to investigate the wrecked ship

nodes.label: Wreck Investigation Prompt
nodes.description: Varnoth and the kobold Rix report a recent shipwreck, hinting at supernatural causes.

nodes.label: Dialogue About Dream
nodes.description: Runara discusses the cleric's dream and suggests zombies relate to the hunger of death

nodes.label: Destiny Discussion Upon Arrival
nodes.description: Character discusses destiny with Runara upon arrival

nodes.label: Destiny Reflection After Quests
nodes.description: Runara prompts character to consider heroic actions shaping destiny

nodes.label: Final Encouragement
nodes.description: Runara assures character destiny will become clearer

nodes.label: Initial Encounter
nodes.description: Paladin arrives in Dragon's Rest and meets Runara.

nodes.label: Guidance from Elders
nodes.description: Runara directs the paladin to consult Tarak and Varnoth about overcoming corruption.

nodes.label: Temple Reflection
nodes.description: Paladin spends time meditating in the temple of Bahamut.

nodes.label: Final Counsel
nodes.description: Runara questions the paladin about lessons learned against corruption.

nodes.label: Dialogue About Past Betrayal
nodes.description: Tarak recounts his betrayal of the guild and tragic love affair, advising the rogue to abandon crime.

nodes.label: Guardian Attack
nodes.description: Three Fume Drakes emerge from the hot spring waters to attack intruders attempting to collect mushrooms or access the spring.

nodes.label: Magical_Regeneration_Benefit
nodes.description: Bathing in the spring grants temporary hit point recovery through Hit Dice rolls

nodes.label: Level Adjustment Notice
nodes.description: Text notes level-based difficulty adjustments for gameplay progression

nodes.label: Blight_Spread_Discovery
nodes.description: A blight originating from the tomb of Sharruth has poisoned the myconid colonies, killing their leader Sinensa.

nodes.label: Vent_Blockade_Event
nodes.description: Blocked vents near Seagrow Caves caused toxic fumes from Sharruth's tomb to spill into the myconid caves.

nodes.label: Stirge_Encounter
nodes.description: Blood-sucking stirges nest in the caves, posing a danger to intruders despite avoiding the fungus-eating myconids.

nodes.label: Travel Options
nodes.description: Characters select between boating or hiking routes to reach Seagrow Caves

nodes.label: Tide_Cycle_Description
nodes.description: Text details tidal patterns affecting access to the caves

nodes.label: Guardian Encounter
nodes.description: A spore servant octopus ambushes intruders in the entrance tunnel.

nodes.label: Stirge_Join_Battle
nodes.description: Two stirge cling to the tunnel ceiling and join the战斗 if fighting breaks out in this area.

nodes.label: Discovery
nodes.description: A sentient mushroom emerges from the ground with pulsating tendrils

nodes.label: Fungal Ambush
nodes.description: Three violet fungi ambush the party, with two remaining stationary until provoked

nodes.label: Environmental Hazard
nodes.description: Characters discover the deceptive appearance of non-threatening purple fungi

nodes.label: Encountering Stirges
nodes.description: Two stirges are added to the encounter for 2nd-level characters.

nodes.label: Fire_Snake_Egg_Discovery
nodes.description: Adventurers discover 25 obsidian fragments worth 10 gp each from a shattered fire snake egg

nodes.label: Sinensa Grants Permission
nodes.description: Sinensa awards the party with the ruby morel and permits retention of other loot

nodes.label: Elixir Creation
nodes.description: Tarak reveals method to craft elixir of health using ruby morel

nodes.label: Zombie_Encounters_in_Wrecks
nodes.description: Characters exploring other wrecks discover additional zombies animated by the talisman in Compass Rose's hold.

nodes.label: Giant_Octopus_Encounter
nodes.description: Giant octopuses ambush explorers in submerged wreckage.

nodes.label: Ghoul_Danger_Aboard
nodes.description: Unusual dangers like ghouls lurk within other shipwrecks.

nodes.label: Description_of_Wreck_Structure
nodes.description: Text describes structural features of the shipwreck including walls, ceilings, doors, and lighting conditions.

nodes.label: Exploration
nodes.description: Adventurers observe a derelict ship wedged against rock formations and massive dragon bones

nodes.label: Exploration
nodes.description: Adventurers explore the main deck of the shipwreck, noting environmental details and discovering potential access points below.

nodes.label: Treasure_Discovery
nodes.description: Characters discover valuable loot in the crow's nest including jewelry and gems

nodes.label: Broken Ballista
nodes.description: The ballista is found to be nonfunctional

nodes.label: Observation
nodes.description: Small, harmless crabs crawl over skeletal remains, creating an illusion of movement

nodes.label: Suspicious_Movement
nodes.description: The skeleton appears to move unexpectedly

nodes.label: Floorboard Trap Activation
nodes.description: Triggering the floorboard releases a dart trap with an attack bonus of +5, dealing 2 (1d4) piercing damage and requiring a 11 Constitution saving throw against poison damage.

nodes.label: Environmental Guidance
nodes.description: Text references climbing/swimming mechanics and breathable surfaces in the submerged hold

nodes.label: Exploration
nodes.description: Characters plan to explore the Clifftop Observatory via various routes

nodes.label: Winged Shape Observed
nodes.description: Adventurers spot a winged, glittering blue shape swooping overhead after hearing a screeching roar

nodes.label: Exploration
nodes.description: Characters explore cliffs leading to area D1

nodes.label: Exploration
nodes.description: Characters explore platforms atop pillars (D2-D5)

nodes.label: ConsiderApproachOptions
nodes.description: Evaluating methods to ascend cliffs or climb to ruins

nodes.label: SpotDragonWyrmling
nodes.description: A glowing blue dragon wyrmling is spotted resting on a distant column

nodes.label: Bridge Activation
nodes.description: Using the moonstone key activates an energy bridge across the gap

nodes.label: Exploration Challenge
nodes.description: Characters seek creative solutions to access the isolated study

nodes.label: Dragon Encounter Choice
nodes.description: Players must choose between battling Sparkrender or navigating to the secret library

nodes.label: Potential Rescue Mission
nodes.description: Option to free the imprisoned Bronze Dragon Wyrmling

nodes.label: Presence of Blue Dragon
nodes.description: A blue dragon wyrmling is found sleeping in the northeast corner of the tower

nodes.label: Avoid Awakening Dragon
nodes.description: Characters attempt silent movement to bypass dragon detection

nodes.label: Statue Interaction Challenge
nodes.description: Statues require stealth checks to prevent waking Sparkrender

nodes.label: Freeing Aidron
nodes.description: Aidron is bound by chains requiring three clasps to be removed, reducing his movement after liberation.

nodes.label: Creature Listing
nodes.description: Adventure features these creatures in its lore

edges.desc: Ancient cloister where dragons once clashed
Submerged vent linked to Sharruth's imprisonment
Vent in cloister
Hidden vent accessed via subterranean pathways
Vent in Seagrow Caves
Seagrow Caves on Stormwreck Isle
Vent in submerged hold
Cabin located within submerged hold
Submerged hold within lower deck
Submerged chamber in the ruins
Ruined tower within area D5
Secret library accessible via caved-in wall beneath tower
Accessible area near the secret library in the tower
Lives in the cloister
Guardians at the site of a brass dragon's death (implied through association with the vent, though not explicitly stated; however, due to lack of clearer connections, this remains tentative)
Leads the cloister of Dragon's Rest
Zombified sailors use the Dash item to move closer during combat
Zombies are present at the vent location
Elder Runara is the leader of the cloister
Kobolds are present in the cloister
Myla is referenced in the kobolds section (implied association)
Zombies are implied to be guarding the cloister's perimeter (contextual inference from defeating zombies at the beach)
The group of kobolds includes individual kobolds
Secret lair accessed via underwater tunnel
Utterly devoted to Runara
Implied protection of the cloister (via Agga’s role)
Tends the cloister's garden plots
Fungus-covered octopus monster guards the sea caves (implied connection through Tarak's failed attempts to access them)
Spending her twilight years in reflective contemplation
Uses to maintain the temple and other areas of the cloister
Myla's cell is within the cloister
Cooks on special days, making them everyone’s favorite
Residents of the cloister help her
Help maintain the temple
Help maintain the temple
Gives them a foul-smelling sack of food scraps
Stormed away from the cloister
Taught him peace and studied with him at Dragon's Rest
Rejects peaceful teaching of Runara
Rejects tyrannical rule of Sparkrender
Includes winged kobold
Serve mighty Sharruth
Lurks below in winding tunnels
Nest in the caves
Noxious fumes from Sharruth's tomb filter through the vent
Food scraps from the cloister nourish the fungi in the caves
Fumes from Sharruth's tomb spill into the myconids' caves via the vent
Tarak's preferred method involves taking the rowboat
Attacks any creatures (except myconids) who enter the tunnel
Lurks in the water of the tunnel
Created by the myconid leader to keep outsiders away (implied inverse of "guards")
Clinging to the tunnel ceiling
Two Stirge are part of the group
Released from the crystal at the vent
Used to break the crystal blocking the fissure (implied association with the vent's location)
Uses ruby morel to make and gives to characters
Gives ruby morel to characters (indirectly linked to elixir_of_health through Tarak’s actions)
Settled into the wreck of Compass Rose
Imbued with magic in the ship's hold
Trapped in the wreck for years
Menacing the people of Dragon's Rest (implied association)
Zombies are found in submerged portions of other wrecks
Giant octopuses are found in submerged portions of other wrecks
Ghouls are found in submerged portions of other wrecks
Zombies are sailors killed in wrecks
The rowboat is stored at the ship dock
The rowboat can be used to access the hold
The hold can be reached via the rowboat
The zombie is in area C4
Engraved with mother-of-pearl in the wheel
Aimlessly shuffling around the cabin
Two drowned sailors are found inside the cabin
Implied presence of submerged hold beneath the cabin (explicit textual inference required?)
A faded, painted portrait hangs on one wall.
Lurks in the aft part of the hold
Found in the chest
Found in the chest
Held inside the pouch
Encased in a floating packet from the chest
Implied by the journal’s reference to the talisman in the submerged_hold context
Detect magic identifies the talisman’s necromancy school
Sailors are implicated as carriers of the talisman, referencing Aleitha’s association with the item during the incident.
Makes its lair in the crow's nest (implied to be part of the ship's structure)
Confronts characters on the main deck, suggesting protective/defensive role
Implicitly tied to the submerged hold as the solution to the zombie problem
Home to Sparkrender
Recruits kobolds to join his cause
Descendant of Runara's ancient rival
Left Runara's tutelage
Imprisoned in observatory ruins
Recruited kobolds to join his cause
Returns to his hoard among the ruins
Comes to rest on a rocky spire off the coast near the ruins
Joined forces with Sparkrender
Have served Sparkrender for about a year
Revere Sparkrender as a semidivine figure
Reside in the ruins
Contains a clue to unlock the hidden entrance in the observatory tower (area D5)
Used to scratch the rune and disarm the trap, allowing safe opening of the journal
Requires disarming a magical trap to open, linking to the observatory tower (area D5)
Found in the ruined tower (implied by contextual narrative)
Found in a hidden compartment
Contains 10 gp
Bronze Dragon Wyrmling is imprisoned in the secret library
Sparkrender becomes hostile if Aidron is mentioned
Sparkrender takes the Disengage action, indicating possession of the item
Sparkrender references Tiamat as a divine authority in its dialogue
Sparkrender's hoard includes blue quartz
Sparkrender's hoard includes jasper
Sparkrender trapped Aidron here
Resides in the secret library
Conflicts with Sparkrender due to his threatening intentions
Found in the room
Found in the room
Reveals the location of the +1 battleaxe
Reveals the location of the hold person item
Imprisoned with chains
Lurking during ritual
Lurking during ritual
Located at the base of the statue
Aidron is bound by three heavy chains
Chains are attached to iron rings via clasps (implied through interaction)
Gives a pearl as a reward
Sparkrender pursues characters for revenge (implied antagonism toward adventurers)
